'''Jacob Jones''' (born 15 February 1999) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sr}} forward for [[Doncaster RLFC|Doncaster]] in the [[RFL Championship]].

He previously played for the [[London Broncos]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]]. He spent time on loan from the Broncos at the [[Coventry Bears]] in [[RFL League 1|League 1]], and later joined Coventry on a permanent basis. He has spent time on loan from [[Leigh Leopards|Leigh]] playing for the [[Widnes Vikings]] and London in the [[2023 RFL Championship|Championship]], as well as the [[Rochdale Hornets]] in [[2023 RFL League 1|League 1]].

==Background==
Jones was born in [[Birmingham, West Midlands|Birmingham]] in England. He attended Loughborough College.<ref>[https://www.loughboroughecho.net/sport/other-sport/trio-land-contracts-london-broncos-12552938 Trio land contracts with London Broncos]</ref>

He played for the Birmingham Bulldogs, Crosfields ARLFC and the [[Midlands Hurricanes]] as a junior.<ref>[https://www.rugby-league.com/article/23865/london-broncos-add-five-academy-stars-to-first-team-squad London Broncos add five academy stars to first team squad]</ref>

==Playing career==
===London Broncos===
Jones joined the [[London Broncos]] at the start of the [[2017 RFL Championship season|2017 season]].<ref>[https://www.loughboroughecho.net/sport/other-sport/trio-land-contracts-london-broncos-12552938 Trio land contracts with London Broncos]</ref>

===Coventry Bears===
Jones spent time on loan from London at League 1 club  [[Coventry Bears]] in [[2017 RFL League 1|2017]]. He again played for Coventry on loan from the Broncos in [[2018 RFL League 1|League 1|2018]].

He joined the Coventry Bears on a full-time basis ahead of the [[2019 RFL League 1|2019 season]] whilst studying at [[Loughborough University]].<ref>[https://www.coventrybears.com/blog/bears-sign-jacob-jones BEARS SIGN JACOB JONES]</ref> He played for England Universities.<ref>[https://www.scotlandrl.com/2019/06/29/england-overpower-scotland-to-claim-title/ England overpower Scotland to claim title]</ref> He was nominated for the League One young player of the year award at the end of the season.<ref>[https://www.coventrybears.com/blog/w1b6pawjli3pibdruhd4b1qrgoge1s Jacob Jones Nominated in League One Awards]</ref>

Jones was offered another contract at the end of the 2020 season, but left to join the full-time professional London Broncos.<ref>[https://www.totalrl.com/betfred-league-1-news-round-up-46/ COVENTRY BEARS]</ref>

===London Broncos===
Jones joined the London Broncos first-team squad for the [[2021 RFL Championship|2021 season]].<ref>[https://www.totalrl.com/betfred-league-1-news-round-up-46/ COVENTRY BEARS]</ref> He made his debut for the Broncos in 2021 against the [[Keighley Cougars]].<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://londonbroncosrl.com/2021/03/21/broncos-make-winning-start/ |title=Broncos make winning start |access-date=2024-02-24 |archive-date=2023-11-10 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231110181011/https://londonbroncosrl.com/2021/03/21/broncos-make-winning-start/ |url-status=dead }}</ref>

===Leigh Leopards===
He joined the [[Leigh Centurions]] for the [[2022 RFL Championship|2022 season]].

===Widnes Vikings===
Jones spent time on loan from the renamed Leigh Leopards in 2023 at the [[Widnes Vikings]] in the [[2023 RFL Championship|Championship]].<ref>[https://widnesvikings.co.uk/jacob-jones-makes-vikings-loan-move/ Jacob Jones makes Vikings loan move]</ref>

===Rochdale Hornets===
He played for the [[Rochdale Hornets]] on loan from Leigh in 2023.

===London Broncos===
He spent time on loan from the Leopards in 2023 at the London Broncos in the Championship.<ref>[https://leighleopards.co.uk/jacob-jones-heading-to-london-broncos-on-1-month-loan/ JACOB JONES HEADING TO LONDON BRONCOS ON 1 MONTH LOAN]</ref>

Jones returned on loan to the Broncos at the start of the [[2024 Super League season]].<ref>[https://www.totalrl.com/leigh-leopards-back-jacob-jones-joins-super-league-rivals-on-loan/ Leigh Leopards forward Jacob Jones joins Super League rivals on loan]</ref> He made his [[Super League]] début against the [[Catalans Dragons]].<ref>[https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/match/EVP4369768 London Broncos 0-34 Catalans Dragons]</ref>

===Doncaster RLFC===
In November 2024 he signed for [[Doncaster RLFC|Doncaster]] in the RFL Championship on a one-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=14 Nov 2024|author=Drew Darbyshire|title=Doncaster make five new signings including former Super League trio and international duo|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/doncaster-make-five-new-signings-including-former-super-league-trio}}</ref>
